feedback.text: Digital products need protection from their publishers. The companies claim that the product is only a licence and that it can change its parts or overall how and when it wants (including the removal of the game from the buyer’s library). How can a contract be fair if the other party (with its own legal department) is allowed to modify any part arbitrarily and without penalty? Whether the following companies in the sector can also be checked when collecting user data on their own gambling platforms (Battle.net, Epic games service, EA-origin, Ubisoft connect and Playstation network).
